raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally dispute a negative remark on my credit report associated with a Barclays account. Despite receiving written confirmation from Barclays that I am not responsible for the charges in question, the issue remains unresolved. 

In XXXX, I opened a Barclaycard XXXX to purchase a cellphone. I paid all installments on time and maintained a zero balance. Barclays later replaced this with a XXXX, which I never received or activated due to a change of address. During this time, three unauthorized XXXX charges of {$9.00} each were billed to the account. Because the account was set to paperless billing, I was unaware of these charges or the subsequent late fees until I received a collections notice. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I filed a fraud complaint regarding the {$140.00} balance. On XX/XX/XXXX, I paid the full amount to protect my credit score while awaiting a resolution. On XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays sent a letter confirming that the charges were fraudulent and stating that the negative information would be removed from my credit report within 30 days. 

After years of conflicting information from customer service, I received a letter on XX/XX/XXXX, acknowledging the delay in processing my claim and promising a full refund. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, I received a check for only {$9.00}. Most recently, on XX/XX/XXXX, I open another claim for a full amount refund. The Barclays informed me that they can not fix the credit reporting error and instructed me to contact the credit agencies directly. 

I am requesting that this negative event be removed from my credit report immediately, as Barclays has already verified in writing that the charges were fraudulent. Please let me know what documentation is required to finalize this correction.
